video:video.video:title: Palestinian couples celebrate mass wedding in Gaza
video:video.video:description: The celebration of 150 couples, which was funded by Al Fares Al Shahim, a humanitarian aid operation backed by the United Arab Emirates, was a rare glimpse of hope and resilience displayed by Palestinians whose lives have been shattered by two years of war.

video:video.video:title: Tick season seems to be off to a fast start
video:video.video:description: Tick season seems to be off to a fast start, with an unusually high number of bites already reported across the country.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: King Charles on mission to restore UK-US relationship in Washington
video:video.video:description: State visits are always carried out at the request of the British government to cement international relationships.

video:video.video:title: In Rhode Island, nonprofit works to increase tree equity
video:video.video:description: Newport, Rhode Island, has neighborhoods of opulent mansions with lots of mature trees along with neighborhoods of subsidized housing and far fewer trees.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia Museum of Art embraces Rocky Balboa statue's worldwide appeal
video:video.video:description: A new exhibition, “Rising Up: Rocky and the Making of Monuments,” brings the bronze figure of Rocky Balboa inside for the first time, reframing it as both pop culture icon and part of a 2,000-year tradition of boxing imagery.

video:video.video:title: Overnight attack in Dnipro injures at least 14, emergency services say
video:video.video:description: At least 14 people, including one child, have been injured as a result of an overnight attack in Ukraine's Dnipro, State Emergency Service of Ukraine said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills 2 in eastern Pacific
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it launched another strike Friday on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing two people.

video:video.video:title: Kate, Princess of Wales, lays wreath at London's Cenotaph on Anzac Day
video:video.video:description: Kate, the Princess of Wales, laid a wreath on behalf of King Charles III at the Cenotaph in London on Sunday to mark Anzac Day, which commemorates Australians and New Zealanders who died in conflict.

video:video.video:title: Russian drone attack rekindles Ukrainians' memories of 1986 Chernobyl nuclear disaster
video:video.video:description: The Russian drone hit the outer layer of what is known as the New Safe Confinement structure, or NSC, the vast, $2.1 billion arch-like shell that was completed in 2019 to enclose the original, hastily built concrete “sarcophagus” to keep the damaged Reactor No. 4 and its deadly debris from leaking r

video:video.video:title: Anti-war demonstrators in Madrid back Sánchez in confrontation with Trump over war
video:video.video:description: A Pentagon email was recently leaked suggesting that Spain should be expelled from NATO for failing to cooperate with the United States in its war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Iranian FM Araghchi meets Pakistani PM Sharif
video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Pakistan's capital of Islamabad on Friday to take part in the Pakistani-led mediation efforts, and held a series of meetings with Pakistani officials.

video:video.video:title: 'Live our lives for three seconds': Palestinians mourn relatives killed in Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: They were the latest of the almost 800 people killed in Gaza since a fragile ceasefire between Hamas and Israel was put in place last October, according to figures of Hamas-run Gaza’s health ministry.

video:video.video:title: Militants seen moving outside Mali capital amid reports of attacks
video:video.video:description: Islamic militants and separatists attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities on Saturday, in one of the largest coordinated attacks in the country in recent years.

video:video.video:title: Faster than Bolt: Corgis race for glory in Prague
video:video.video:description: Corgi dogs were racing against each other in an Olympic-style event in Prague in the Czech Republic on Saturday. The fastest record was 8.9 seconds for a 100-metre race.

video:video.video:title: Roommate charged with two counts of murder in death, disappearance of two USF students
video:video.video:description: Authorities filed murder charges against the roommate of a Bangladeshi doctoral student who disappeared with his girlfriend from the University of South Florida.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 25
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 25th: Trump called off dispatching envoys in Iran talks; Roommate of missing USF student is charged with two counts of murder; Zelenskyy calls for more talks after Russian strikes kill five in Ukraine; Rocky exhibit debuts in Philadelphia.

video:video.video:title: Lisbon parade celebrates anniversary of Portugal's Carnation Revolution
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of people marched along Lisbon’s Freedom Avenue on Saturday to celebrate the anniversary of the Carnation Revolution.

video:video.video:title: Unmanned cargo spacecraft takes off for International Space Station
video:video.video:description: The unmanned Progress 95 took off on board a Soyuz rocket from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The Roscosmos spacecraft carries food, fuel, and supplies for the crew on board the International Space Station.

video:video.video:title: Online businesses struggle as Iran keeps internet shutdown in place
video:video.video:description: As Amen Khademi applies lipstick to her model before a photo shoot, her mind is occupied with thoughts of how to keep her business alive.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians vote in local elections in West Bank and part of Gaza
video:video.video:description: Palestinians have voted in local elections, the first in two decades in Gaza and the first in the Israeli occupied West Bank since the start of the war in Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Police say Chicago hospital shooting left one officer dead and another critically injured
video:video.video:description: A police officer died and another was critically injured after a shooting at a Chicago hospital on Saturday, according to officials.

video:video.video:title: Correspondents arrive at the White House after Trump announces news conference
video:video.video:description: Dozens of journalists arrived at the White House after President Donald Trump announced a news conference following a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

video:video.video:title: 'Everbody's safe': Head of White House correspondents' association says after security incident
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and other leaders were unhurt in the security incident at the annual White House correspondents' dinner on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Trump says man 'charged' security checkpoint at White House Correspondents dinner
video:video.video:description: He addressed the media at the White House, after he, the First Lady and senior U.S. leaders were evacuated from an annual dinner of White House correspondents on Saturday night after a security incident.

video:video.video:title: Deadly blast leaves chaos on highway as violence escalates in southwestern Colombia
video:video.video:description: At least 14 people were killed when an explosive device detonated as a bus passed by on a highway in southwestern Colombia on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: WATCH: Law enforcement agents escort President Trump after security incident
video:video.video:description: Incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner prompts security response

video:video.video:title: Firefighters tackle flames as officials say Odesa hit by Russian attack overnight
video:video.video:description: Local authorities reported that port infrastructure, industrial facilities and residential buildings were attacked by Russia overnight with one person injured.

video:video.video:title: Shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner: How the event unfolded
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump was unharmed and other top White House officials were evacuated from an annual dinner of the White House Correspondents' Association after a man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby and opened fire.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran presented new offers 10 minutes after he canceled US team’s trip
video:video.video:description: The latest ceasefire talks between the United States and Iran appeared to fail Saturday before they began, as Tehran's top diplomat left Pakistan and President Donald Trump soon afterward said he had told envoys not to travel to Islamabad.

video:video.video:title: GOP's Vivek Ramaswamy weathering demanding primary season in bid for Ohio governorship
video:video.video:description: Republican Vivek Ramaswamy has won the endorsements of President Donald Trump and the state GOP in his race for Ohio governor.

video:video.video:title: Scenes outside address connected to shooting suspect at the White House correspondents dinner
video:video.video:description: Police officers arrived at an address in Torrance, California, connected to Cole Tomas Allen, the shooting suspect at the White House correspondents' dinner attended by President Donald Trump on Saturday night.

video:video.video:title: Trump says being president is a ‘dangerous profession’
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump struck a somber tone as he addressed reporters at the White House, saying being president is “a dangerous profession” and that attempted violence against him is “part of the job.”

video:video.video:title: FBI probe into stolen masterpieces leads to killers, mobsters and a man called Meatball
video:video.video:description: A former FBI investigator discuses the largest art heist in history – the 1990 robbery at the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in Boston where artwork reportedly worth a billion dollars was stolen.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment Trump gets rushed off stage at the White House correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: A man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside the White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner attended by President Donald Trump on Saturday night, charging toward the ballroom in a chaotic encounter with Secret Service agents as guests dived under tables at the sound of shots being

video:video.video:title: Residents forced to evacuate as firefighters continue to battle wildfires in northeast Japan
video:video.video:description: Some 730 hectares (1,804 acres) were ablaze, despite efforts by the Self-Defence Forces and firefighters to bring the flames under control.

video:video.video:title: Sultan of Oman meets Iranian foreign minister in Muscat
video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met on Sunday with the Sultan of Oman, Haitham bin Tariq Al Said, in Muscat.

video:video.video:title: Speaker of Russian state duma meets North Korea's leader Kim as officials visit Pyongyang
video:video.video:description: Russian State Duma Speaker Vyacheslav Volodin said on Sunday that it was a “great honor” to take part in an inauguration ceremony of a military memorial in Pyongyang.

video:video.video:title: Video shows suspect running toward correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner

video:video.video:title: AP bureau chief describes what happened at the White House Correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: The shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner Saturday night unfolded just outside the ballroom holding President Donald Trump, Vice President JD Vance, cabinet members and thousands of dinner guests, disrupting minutes after it began an annual event.

video:video.video:title: Trump says being president is a ‘dangerous profession’
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump struck a somber tone as he addressed reporters at the White House, saying being president is “a dangerous profession” and that attempted violence against him is “part of the job.”

video:video.video:title: Ukrainians pay tribute to Chernobyl 'liquidators' on 40th anniversary of nuclear disaster
video:video.video:description: People streamed in from different parts of the city to place candles on a large radiation hazard symbol as they marked the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster.

video:video.video:title: Sufi mosque on outskirts of Khartoum offered shelter from violence as war raged nearby
video:video.video:description: Sufi faithful performed zikr inside the war-damaged Sheikh GaribAllah Mosque in Omdurman, on the outskirts of the Sudanese capital, on Friday.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter gives first-hand account of the security incident
video:video.video:description: First-hand account of Trump's evacuation after security incident at correspondents' dinner

video:video.video:title: DC mayor says 'no reason to believe' anyone else involved in dinner shooting incident
video:video.video:description: The mayor of Washington D.C. said that authorities had "no reason to believe" anyone else was "involved" after a man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside a high-profile journalists' dinner attended by President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: DC mayor says 'no reason to believe' anyone else involved in dinner shooting incident
video:video.video:description: In a news conference on Saturday, Muriel Bowser thanked law enforcement "who did their jobs tonight and made sure that all guests were safe."

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes security incident at White House correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump was unharmed and other top White House officials were evacuated from an annual dinner of the White House Correspondents' Association after a man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby and opened fire.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 26
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, April 26th: Gunman at WHCD; 1 officer injured, suspect arrested. Iran FM in Islamabad; Pakistan seeks talks, Trump says call possible.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the suspect in the White House Correspondents' dinner incident
video:video.video:description: Investigators have not publicly named the suspect, but two law enforcement officials familiar with the matter have identified him to The Associated Press as 31-year-old Cole Tomas Allen of Torrance, California.

video:video.video:title: Law enforcement respond after shooting incident at White House Correspondents’ dinner
video:video.video:description: A man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby outside the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner attended by President Donald Trump on Saturday night, charging toward the ballroom in a chaotic encounter with Secret Service agents as guests dived under tables at the sound of shots being

video:video.video:title: Phone video shows WH officials and guests rushing out of media dinner
video:video.video:description: A man with guns and knives tried to rush past the security perimeter inside the Washington hotel where President Donald Trump was about to address the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner.

video:video.video:title: South Lebanon residents flee amid Israeli warning to residents of Nabatieh province
video:video.video:description: People fled different villages in southern Lebanon on Sunday amid Israeli warnings to residents of villages in the Nabatieh province.

video:video.video:title: New supersized telescope in Atacama desert could offer view of Earth-like planets
video:video.video:description: In Chile’s Atacama Desert, the night sky feels infinite. Considered the driest place on Earth, its darkness offers one of the clearest windows to the universe.

video:video.video:title: Race car driver Colapinto takes to track in Buenos Aires as Argentines remain hopeful about a possible F1 return
video:video.video:description: Argentine Formula 1 driver Franco Colapinto drew an estimated 500,000 spectators to a street exhibition in Buenos Aires, an event aimed at positioning the city as a potential future host of a Grand Prix.

video:video.video:title: Funeral held after Israeli forces kill four Palestinians in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Funerals were held in Gaza City Sunday for Palestinians killed by Israeli forces hours earlier. At least four people were killed by Israeli forces on Sunday morning in the Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian and Moldovan presidents, IAEA chief mark 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster
video:video.video:description: Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy, his Moldovan counterpart Maia Sandu and IAEA chief Rafael Grossi took part in a ceremony on Sunday and a conference in Chernobyl to mark the 40th anniversary of the nuclear disaster.

video:video.video:title: Older Cubans struggle as the economic crisis deepens
video:video.video:description: A generation of Cubans, now in their 70s and 80s, has been left to fend for itself as the economic crisis deepens.

video:video.video:title: Flooding from rising Al-Siha Dam waters submerges villages in northwest Syria
video:video.video:description: Floodwaters covered agricultural land and homes in parts of northwestern Syria after heavy rains caused water levels to rise at the Al-Siha Dam, affecting several villages, including Hmeimat al-Deir.

video:video.video:title: Chernobyl commemoration ceremony held in Moscow
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people participated in a commemoration ceremony at a Moscow cemetery for those who died from deadly radiation exposure while containing the aftermath of the Chernobyl disaster 40 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Latest US military strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in eastern Pacific
video:video.video:description: The latest U.S. military strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean killed three people Sunday, according to a social media post by U.S.

video:video.video:title: How a surgeon kept a Sudan hospital functioning on the war's front line
video:video.video:description: For three years, Dr. Jamal Eltaeb made excruciating choices. Who should live and potentially die? Should he operate without the right medicines if it might save someone's life?

video:video.video:title: Robot guides art tours in Turin museum
video:video.video:description: A robot is the latest guide at Turin's Palazzo Madama museum. The humanoid machine can tell visitors about the artefacts on display and correct its own behaviour if it comes up against any unexpected challenges.
